faqs.question: Why should I flush my water heater?
faqs.answer: Sediment builds up at the bottom of your tank over time, reducing efficiency and potentially causing damage. Flushing removes this buildup, improving performance and extending the life of your unit.

faqs.question: How often should a water heater be flushed?
faqs.answer: We recommend flushing your water heater at least once a year. Homes with hard water may need it every six months to prevent excessive mineral buildup.

faqs.question: What are signs my water heater needs flushing?
faqs.answer: Common signs include rumbling or popping noises from the tank, rusty or discolored hot water, reduced hot water supply, and higher energy bills than usual.
